item.title: MGM U16 eye Deans national quarters
item.description: RUNNERS up for the under-15 grade of the Fiji Secondary Schools Deans competition last year and now in the U16 grade, Mahatma Gandhi Memorial School have set a goal to reach the national quarter-finals once again this year. This has been the aim for the Vatuwaqa-based school, after it began its Deans campaign in the Southern Zone yesterday.
item.content:encoded:
RUNNERS up for the under-15 grade of the Fiji Secondary Schools Deans competition last year and now in the U16 grade, Mahatma Gandhi Memorial School have set a goal to reach the national quarter-finals once again this year.

This has been the aim for the Vatuwaqa-based school, after it began its Deans campaign in the Southern Zone yesterday.

In a thrilling match on round one of the Southern Zone Deans competitions, the side defeated Gospel High School 36-6 at the Suva Grammar School grounds in Veiuto, Suva.

Team captain Waisiki Ramatakubou said after the match how the team’s preparation had been going well which also led to the school’s eventual victory in the first round of the Southern Zone Deans competition.

Although the team acquired a victory, Ramatakubou says there still much room for improvement specifically on their discipline.

“Our team still needs to work on our discipline. We had one yellow card, so that’s something we need to work on,” the captain, who plays as lock, said.

The team sets their aim for the U16 Southern Zone title and as well as progressing beyond the national quarters.

“We aim to win the U16 title in the southern zones and as well s making it further beyond the national quarters. We came short in the deans final last year, so we are hoping to improve and better this year,” he said.

The captain also expressed gratitude to the parents, coaches, teachers and students for the support shown towards the team.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: SVNS survival
item.description: QUALIFICATION for the next HSBC SVNS 1 season was a key focus for the Fiji Airways Fiji men’s and Fijiana 7s teams as they completed pool matches at the Bordeaux 7s in France. The men’s side must finish with more than 26 points on the World Championship standings—points they entered Bordeaux with—to secure qualification for next season.
item.content:encoded:
QUALIFICATION for the next HSBC SVNS 1 season was a key focus for the Fiji Airways Fiji men’s and Fijiana 7s teams as they completed pool matches at the Bordeaux 7s in France.

The men’s side must finish with more than 26 points on the World Championship standings—points they entered Bordeaux with—to secure qualification for next season.

Fiji Airways Fiji Men’s 7s are currently in a fight to retain top-tier status after finishing fourth in Valladolid, Spain, leaving them on the edge of either qualification retention or relegation to SVNS 2.

Argentina, Australia and South Africa have already secured qualification after top-three finishes in the standings prior to the Bordeaux leg.

The Fijian men boosted their campaign with pool wins over Kenya (31-12) and Great Britain (26-12). They met South Africa in their final pool match at 9.06pm last night.

For the women, the Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s sit seventh in the standings and must work harder to secure qualification for next season’s elite series.

The Fijiana needed to reach the quarter-finals to strengthen their chances, after mixed results against Australia (7-19) and a 24-12 win over Japan, with their final pool match against Brazil proving decisive.

New Zealand, Australia, the United States and France have already qualified for next season’s HSBC SVNS series.

Fiji’s performance in the SVNS Series from Dubai in November to New York in February helped secure their place in the World Championships.

The World Championships are played across three legs, with only the top eight teams from the SVNS Series qualifying for Hong Kong, Valladolid and Bordeaux.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Ba Methodist upsets visitors
item.description: BA Methodist High School U18 edged Tonga High School 28–14 in an entertaining encounter at the Pacific Schools Rugby League Championship at Churchill Park yesterday. Ba Methodist made a strong statement early, dominating the first half with structured attack and solid defence to take a commanding 20–4 lead into halftime.
item.content:encoded:
BA Methodist High School U18 edged Tonga High School 28–14 in an entertaining encounter at the Pacific Schools Rugby League Championship at Churchill Park yesterday.

Ba Methodist made a strong statement early, dominating the first half with structured attack and solid defence to take a commanding 20–4 lead into halftime.

The Ba side struck first through centre Ratu Osea Naula before Aporosa Ragata crossed for their second try. Isoa Nukunidreke added the third try as Ba Methodist controlled possession and territory.

Halfback Taniela Kunarara had a perfect first-half kicking display, landing three conversions and adding a penalty to extend the lead. Tonga High School managed only one first-half try through Aloha Tuakalau but failed to convert.

Trailing by 16 points at the break, Tonga High came out with increased intensity in the second half. Their pressure paid off when Soane Lehapoto scored their second try, with the conversion successfully added to reduce the deficit.

Despite sustained pressure from the Tongan side, Ba Methodist held firm in defence during key moments and capitalised on their chances late in the match.

Pio Tikicidre scored Ba’s fourth try in the final quarter, which was converted by Kunarara to further extend their lead.

Tonga continued to fight back and added a third try through Pinoni Tuitupou, though the conversion was unsuccessful. However, Ba Methodist sealed the match with another penalty from Kunarara in the dying minutes to secure a 28–14 victory.

In the girls’ match, Tonga Junior Girls defeated Ratu Latianara College 36–0.

Ba Methodist coach David Wise praised his team’s unity after the match, saying their strong bond played a key role in the win.

“It’s our team bonding that has kept us together,” he said.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Lions eye repeat
item.description: THE last time Labasa hosted and won the Fiji FACT on home soil, it was 1992. More than three decades later, the memories still live strongly in the North.
item.content:encoded:
THE last time Labasa hosted and won the Fiji FACT on home soil, it was 1992.

More than three decades later, the memories still live strongly in the North.

Now, in 2026, Labasa FC has another chance to create history.

As the BiC Fiji FACT returns to Subrail Park this month, the defending champions are not only chasing another title but also the opportunity to become back-to-back winners in front of their home fans.

Labasa is pooled in Group A with Rewa, Suva and Navua.

For club president Shaheen Hussain, the build-up to the tournament carries both excitement and pressure.

Hussain took over the leadership role from long-serving administrator Reyaz Khan and now finds himself guiding one of the country’s most passionate football districts into another major campaign.

Labasa enters this year’s Fiji FACT carrying momentum after a strong start to the domestic season.

Despite losing several key players following last year’s title-winning campaign, the side has remained among the competition’s most consistent teams in the Extra Premier League,

But beyond football, the return of the Fiji FACT at Labasa is expected to once again transform the town.

And when the final whistle blows at Subrail Park, one of Fiji’s loudest football venues is expected to come alive once again.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Reds retain title
item.description: THE Dynamic Reds women’s basketball side have retained their title once again in the 2026 Basketball Fiji Vodafone Cup championship for the women’s division after a 38-28 victory against the Kaunikula Royals yesterday. It was a tight match at the FMF Gymnasium in Suva against the Kaunikuila Royals with the points being 15-15 in the second quarter.
item.content:encoded:
THE Dynamic Reds women’s basketball side have retained their title once again in the 2026 Basketball Fiji Vodafone Cup championship for the women’s division after a 38-28 victory against the Kaunikula Royals yesterday.

It was a tight match at the FMF Gymnasium in Suva against the Kaunikuila Royals with the points being 15-15 in the second quarter.

The Dynamic Reds team captain Vika Kuboutawa said the team was told during the break to regroup and reflect on their mistakes which led to their victory.

“In the second quarter, on the bench, coach told us that we need to re-evaluate and reflect on our mistakes and work on it,” Kuboutawa said.

Kuboutawa added the victory was a result of their team dedication, commitment and sacrifice in the preparation to the Vodafone Cup championship.

With late evening trainings and Saturday clinics, the team’s victory was a reward for their hard work over the past weeks of preparation.

“We’ve always had training in the evening from 6pm till late.”

“Most of our young players are part of the Saturday clinics programme and it is great seeing them step up and play for the Dynamic Reds team.”

The championship provided crucial national-level exposure for the team’s developing players as she expressed how the team would continue its development.

Kuboutawa also acknowledged the supporters from the Raiwaqa community and overseas, which boosted the team’s performance for the competition.

“We are thankful to our supporters from the Raiwaqa community who turned out in numbers and also the supporters from overseas who have also been a strong source of encouragement to the team.”

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Boston Redz retains Fiji Cup basketball title
item.description: THE Boston Redz men’s team successfully defended their title after defeating Kaunikuila Islanders 25-14 in a thrilling Basketball Fiji Vodafone Cup Championship final at the FMF Gymnasium in Suva yesterday. Head coach Jordan Dokonivalu said the team had prepared thoroughly for the championship decider, knowing the Islanders would pose a serious challenge.
item.content:encoded:
THE Boston Redz men’s team successfully defended their title after defeating Kaunikuila Islanders 25-14 in a thrilling Basketball Fiji Vodafone Cup Championship final at the FMF Gymnasium in Suva yesterday.

Head coach Jordan Dokonivalu said the team had prepared thoroughly for the championship decider, knowing the Islanders would pose a serious challenge.

“After meeting the Islanders in our opening pool match, we knew they would come back stronger in the final,” Dokonivalu told Times Sport.

“We had seen their attacking style of play, so our main focus was strengthening our defence heading into the final.”

Dokonivalu said retaining the title was particularly significant for the Raiwaqa community, as the team entered the tournament carrying the weight of expectations as defending champions.

“We thank God for His provision in allowing us to defend the title. As champions, we knew every team came into the tournament with the goal of beating us, so this victory means a lot to our players and supporters,” he said.

With the scores close at halftime, Dokonivalu reminded his players of the values that had carried them throughout the competition.

“During the break, I told the boys not to lose sight of teamwork, communication and unity because those are the things that would help us win. They responded well and executed the game plan in the second half.”

The title-winning coach said the team’s attention would now turn to international duties, with several players set to represent Fiji at the upcoming Asia Cup Championship.

“After the Fiji Cup, our focus shifts to the Asia Cup where a number of our players are part of the national squad, so we are looking forward to that challenge.”

Dokonivalu also acknowledged the unwavering support of parents, families, sponsors, well-wishers and the Raiwaqa community throughout the tournament.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Ablaze Netball aims to build on success
item.description: ABLAZE Hearts Netball Club is looking to continue its strong development pathway and competitive success this season at the NewWorld IGA Suva Netball Competition. Club coach Filomena Korovulavula said the season had started positively, with players eager to return to the courts after the opening rounds of competition.
item.content:encoded:
ABLAZE Hearts Netball Club is looking to continue its strong development pathway and competitive success this season at the NewWorld IGA Suva Netball Competition.

Club coach Filomena Korovulavula said the season had started positively, with players eager to return to the courts after the opening rounds of competition.

“The season is going really well. We just started last week and again this week, and we’re looking forward to a good year for Ablaze,” she said.

The club has entered 15 teams this season and welcomed many new players into its ranks.

Korovulavula said the club was aiming to build on last year’s success after winning the Roses Grade competition and remained hopeful of achieving more this season.

A passionate advocate for youth development says helping young players reach their potential remained her greatest motivation.

“This is what I love. Developing the young ones is a passion for me. I enjoy helping them improve every training session,” she said.

Korovulavula believes netball continues to provide valuable opportunities for young athletes, with many players using club competitions as preparation for secondary school tournaments and higher representative honours.

The club is also preparing for a tour to Australia next month, where three teams will compete against overseas opposition.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Timely boost for Ba FC
item.description: BA FC has received a timely boost ahead of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT after Ba FC secured a major sponsorship deal with Roylux Realty. Under the agreement, the western giants will compete under the name Roylux Realty Ba FC during the tournament, which will be staged at Subrail Park from June 19–21. The semi-finals will be held on June 26, with the final scheduled for June 27.
item.content:encoded:
BA FC has received a timely boost ahead of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT after Ba FC secured a major sponsorship deal with Roylux Realty.

Under the agreement, the western giants will compete under the name Roylux Realty Ba FC during the tournament, which will be staged at Subrail Park from June 19–21. The semi-finals will be held on June 26, with the final scheduled for June 27.

The partnership comes at a crucial time as Ba intensifies its preparations for one of Fiji football’s most prestigious competitions.

Ba FC president Praneel Dayal expressed gratitude to Roylux Realty for its support and belief in the club.

“It gives me great pleasure to announce Roylux Realty as the official major sponsor for Ba FC in the 2026 BIC Fiji FACT “When you think of excellence, leadership in the real estate sector, you think of Roylux Realty. They are a household name and the number one choice when it comes to buying, selling land or commercial spaces.

“What makes this partnership special is that Roylux Realty is a proud company based in Lautoka, yet their managing director, Mr Kishan Naidu, has chosen to put their resources behind the champion Ba brand.

“We are grateful to Roylux Realty for coming on board as our major sponsor. Their support is a huge boost for the team and reflects their belief in the vision and direction of Ba FC. This partnership will help us prepare well and compete at the highest level.”

Dayal added that the club remains committed to delivering strong performances and making its supporters proud at the upcoming tournament.

Roylux Realty managing director Kishan Naidu said the company’s support for Ba FC is driven by a belief in investing in people and sport development.

“We at Roylux Realty believe in investing in people and supporting sports. We trust Ba football,” Naidu said.

“It’s not just this year we are supporting Ba, but in the years to come.

“As the president said, I am from Lautoka, but why support Ba? We all have our own favourite districts and colours, but Ba has always portrayed good and skilled football. We have watched legends come and go, but the name still remains. So we are here on trust, and we know Ba will deliver.

“We stand by Ba, and we would like to thank the fans for always supporting Ba. Thank you Mr President, and let’s wish the team all the best for the Fiji FACT.”

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.
